[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.74) — 12 imports target "web-viewer"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.26) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [info] Applying the two-consumer rule before adding new files here will prevent further cohesion erosion in an already fragile zone.
[observation] [warning] Cohesion (0.26) is well below the 0.4 warning threshold and coupling (0.74) exceeds 0.6 — this zone meets the dual-fragility criteria requiring active governance on additions.
[observation] [warning] The prd.ts view is the sole declared entry point, but use-toast and use-feature-toggle are likely consumed outside this zone too — verify via import graph to determine whether they should be promoted to the viewer hub.
[pattern] [info] Moving use-toast and use-feature-toggle into web-viewer hub would dissolve two of the four files from this dual-fragility zone, reducing it to prd.ts + bulk-actions.ts as a focused PRD-editing unit. This would mechanically raise cohesion and lower coupling without requiring any logic changes, since both hooks have zero external zone imports.
[relationship] [warning] use-toast and use-feature-toggle have zero external imports and are general-purpose hooks that could be consumed by any viewer zone. Currently there is no boundary-check assertion requiring consumers to access them through a barrel. As the hook count grows, add a barrel enforcement assertion analogous to the crash/ sub-zone check to prevent silent coupling growth.
[anti-pattern] [warning] viewer-prd-interaction contains structurally incompatible file archetypes: a page-level route component (prd.ts, singleton by definition) and reusable utilities (use-toast, use-feature-toggle, bulk-actions.ts). Pages are composition roots that can never satisfy the two-consumer rule; utilities must satisfy it. Mixing both archetypes in a dual-fragility zone makes zone-level governance unenforceable — the correct fix is full zone dissolution into web-viewer rather than partial hook extraction that leaves a 2-file residual zone still below the reliable-metric threshold.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "Viewer PRD Interaction Layer" (viewer-prd-interaction) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.74, cohesion: 0.26, coupling: 0.74) — unreliable: zone has only 4 files (minimum 5 for reliable metrics)

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.26) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.74) — 12 imports target "web-viewer"
- Low cohesion (0.26) and high coupling (0.74) reflect mixed responsibilities: a page-level view, a complex component, and two reusable hooks were pulled together by shared import patterns rather than domain coherence.
- The 12 outbound imports to Web Viewer Hub and 5 to Web Dashboard Platform make this zone a coupling hotspot; splitting hooks into the viewer hub and keeping prd.ts + bulk-actions.ts as a focused 'PRD editing' unit would improve both metrics.
- use-toast and use-feature-toggle are general-purpose hooks used beyond the PRD view — confirm they belong here or whether they should live closer to their broadest consumer.
- Cohesion (0.26) is well below the 0.4 warning threshold and coupling (0.74) exceeds 0.6 — this zone meets the dual-fragility criteria requiring active governance on additions.
- The prd.ts view is the sole declared entry point, but use-toast and use-feature-toggle are likely consumed outside this zone too — verify via import graph to determine whether they should be promoted to the viewer hub.
- Applying the two-consumer rule before adding new files here will prevent further cohesion erosion in an already fragile zone.
- use-toast and use-feature-toggle are zero-dependency hooks (only preact/hooks imports) — they impose no cross-zone coupling from their side and are safe to relocate to web-viewer without introducing cycles. Relocating them would reduce viewer-prd-interaction's file count, raising its cohesion and reducing coupling.
- There is no boundary-check.test.ts assertion governing how viewer-prd-interaction modules are consumed. Unlike crash/ (which has barrel enforcement) and shared/ (which has two-consumer enforcement), viewer-prd-interaction has no automated guardrail preventing leaf-file imports of use-toast or use-feature-toggle from external zones.
- use-toast and use-feature-toggle have zero external imports and are general-purpose hooks that could be consumed by any viewer zone. Currently there is no boundary-check assertion requiring consumers to access them through a barrel. As the hook count grows, add a barrel enforcement assertion analogous to the crash/ sub-zone check to prevent silent coupling growth.
- Moving use-toast and use-feature-toggle into web-viewer hub would dissolve two of the four files from this dual-fragility zone, reducing it to prd.ts + bulk-actions.ts as a focused PRD-editing unit. This would mechanically raise cohesion and lower coupling without requiring any logic changes, since both hooks have zero external zone imports.
- prd.ts is a route-level page component — by definition a singleton with a single consumer (the router). It structurally cannot satisfy the two-consumer rule that governs this dual-fragility zone. A page file is a composition root, not a shared module, and requires different governance treatment than the utility hooks co-located with it. Mixing page-level singletons and reusable utilities in the same zone makes the two-consumer rule unenforceable on the zone as a whole.
- After relocating use-toast and use-feature-toggle to web-viewer, the residual zone (prd.ts + bulk-actions.ts) would still be only 2 files — still below the 5-file reliable-metric threshold. The post-relocation zone would recreate the same micro-zone metrics problem in a smaller form. Full dissolution into web-viewer rather than partial extraction avoids producing a new metrics-unreliable micro-zone as a side effect.
- viewer-prd-interaction contains structurally incompatible file archetypes: a page-level route component (prd.ts, singleton by definition) and reusable utilities (use-toast, use-feature-toggle, bulk-actions.ts). Pages are composition roots that can never satisfy the two-consumer rule; utilities must satisfy it. Mixing both archetypes in a dual-fragility zone makes zone-level governance unenforceable — the correct fix is full zone dissolution into web-viewer rather than partial hook extraction that leaves a 2-file residual zone still below the reliable-metric threshold.
- [call graph] 2 internal calls, 8 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 0.2, coupling: 0.8)

</insights>
